name: Lime-Marinated Chicken Breasts
recipeIngredient: 2 whole chicken breasts without skin, boneless; halved
6 ounces pea pods, fresh or frozen
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound each chicken breast half between 2 pieces of plastic wrap to a 1/4 inch thickness. Remove the plastic wrap.
2. Arrange the chicken breasts in a shallow baking dish.
3. For the marinade, combine soy sauce, lime juice, water, olive oil, grated ginger, and minced garlic in a bowl.
4. Pour the marinade over the chicken breasts. Cover and refrigerate for 3 to 24 hours.
5. Before serving, remove the chicken breasts from the marinade and reserve it.
6. Place the chicken on a rack above an unheated broiler pan.
7. Broil the chicken 4-5 inches from the heat for 6 to 8 minutes, turning once.
8. Meanwhile, add water to the reserved marinade to make 1/3 cup liquid. In a saucepan, stir in the cornstarch and cook until thickened and bubbly, stirring for an additional 3 minutes.
9. Stir in the frozen pea pods (use fresh peas if available) and continue cooking for 3 more minutes, stirring once.
10. Serve the sauce over the broiled chicken breasts. Makes 4 servings.
